title: archangelica officinalis var. sativa
intro: Archangelica officinalis var. sativa, also known as wild angelica, is a biennial plant of the carrot family (Apiaceae) that grows up to 2 meters in height and is native to Europe. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, making it effective against coughs, colds, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of wild angelica include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and even help to treat anxiety and insomnia. Wild angelica can be consumed as an herbal tea, infused in oil for topical use, or made into a tincture for medicinal purposes.
